expected_outcome: Fine-tuned splade-ecommerce-esci returns product results (likely supplements, energy drinks, or biology textbooks). This isn't model failure — it's corpus mismatch: a product-search system returns products, regardless of intent. In production, route general-knowledge queries to a different system upstream.

Fine-tuned model returns programming books, Raspberry Pi kits, or unrelated products driven by the token 'python' in retail context. The corpus has no docs/tutorials, so the model can't return them — by design. Use query routing or a separate documentation index for this intent.

Fine-tuned model returns history books, WWII memorabilia, or military-themed apparel — the product catalog has no factual answers to surface. The model is doing its job (retrieving products); the system around it needs to recognize the query isn't shopping and route accordingly.

Fine-tuned model returns bread machines, sourdough starter kits, baking pans, and cookbooks — superficially plausible because 'shopping for sourdough supplies' is a real intent, but the user actually wants a recipe. A query classifier upstream would catch the 'how to' pattern and route to instructions, not products.

text: what is the capital of mongolia
expected_outcome: Fine-tuned model returns travel guides, Mongolia-themed decor, or products triggered by 'capital' (letter stencils, financial books). The model retrieves from what it has — products. Factual lookups belong in a different system; this is a query-routing problem, not a model problem.
